near_leaf_pairs.note: Same broad EM-debt call target on adjacent leaves (Fixed Income - Sovereigns vs Fixed Income - Geo). Views conflict because the model read the p.8 EM ex China/Russia 10-yr forecast falling 8.85->8.51 as O, while GT read the p.8-9 prose (inflation transmission, easing priced out, capital outflows, stagflation risk) as U. Table-delta vs prose-stance convention conflict.

view_mismatch_judgments.reason: p.8 shows AB's own 10-yr JGB forecast flat at 2.25% for 2026 and 2027 (model's N is faithful to that), while the same page's prose says the BOJ will raise rates again with the policy rate going 1.00->1.25 (GT's U is faithful to that) — the source itself is neutral on the 10-yr but hawkish on policy.

AB's own table forecasts the US 10-yr flat at 4.00% both years (p.4/p.10, supporting N), yet the p.3 central narrative explicitly says markets price too much tightening and 'yields down over time' and p.4-5 says the next Fed move is a cut (supporting O); notably the model itself credited that prose to a separate 'Duration' O row, so both readings are drawn from the document.

The p.10 Europe aggregate long-rate forecast falls 3.37->2.90 (a 2027-horizon decline supporting O), while the p.6-7 prose stance is ECB on hold at neutral with increased near-term hike risk and AB skeptical of aggressive hikes (supporting N) — a horizon/convention difference, not a factual error by either side.

model_only_judgments.reason: p.2 prose shares 'some of the concern' on private-market opacity/excesses but explicitly concludes issues are 'unlikely to trigger a systemic crisis' and 'this does not look like 2008/2009' — a risk note, not a directional allocation call; UNCERTAIN would have been the correct code.

Directly grounded in the p.3 central narrative ('markets likely to price too much tightening... should bring yields down over time') — a real global duration-positive house view that GT expressed only via its US Treasuries O row.

Rests solely on the table's 10-yr forecast rising 2.00->2.25 (p.5); a real 25bp delta consistently applied under the model's yield-delta convention, but no prose bond view exists — whether a forecast delta constitutes a 'call' is the disputed convention.

Table forecasts CNY 7.10->7.20 depreciation (p.5) and the direction is corroborated by p.9 prose on a stronger USD and EM currency depreciation; a legitimate call GT covered only via the broader EM Basket row.

Table shows Euro Area 10-yr 3.00->2.60 (p.6) supporting O, but the prose stance GT used for its adjacent Europe Duration N row (ECB hold with hike risk) points neutral — the same table-vs-prose conflict as the Europe Duration mismatch, duplicated on a second leaf.

A ~1-cent EUR/USD forecast move (1.16->1.17) is too small to imply a stance and directly contradicts the document's explicit USD safe-haven strength view (p.3), which the model itself emitted as USD O.

The -70bp forecast decline (4.80->4.10, p.7) is material, but the p.7-8 prose stance is BoE 'on hold for an extended period' with cuts only conditionally resuming Q4 2026 — the O rests entirely on the 2027 table delta, not a stated stance.
Table forecasts GBP 1.34->1.32 (p.7) and the direction is backed by the p.3 USD safe-haven prose (GT itself holds USD O); a small but coherent call GT did not enumerate.
A 4bp delta (5.71->5.67) is noise, not a direction; and the p.8-9 prose is explicitly cautious on oil-importing Asian economies, pointing the other way.
A 14bp delta on a 9.90% base is immaterial and conflicts with the document's cautious EM prose (easing priced out, stagflation risk).

The -13bp table delta alone would be thin, but the call matches the explicit p.3 'yields down over time' central narrative; note it is the same underlying call as the model's Duration row, emitted on a second leaf.

The Industrial Countries aggregate (3.63->3.43, p.10) is a third rendering of the same global-duration call (after Duration and Global Govt Bonds/SSAs); table-aggregate enumeration rather than a distinct house view.
Purely table-derived (3.25->3.00, p.10) with zero Canada prose in the document; a real 25bp delta but a 'call' only under the delta-as-view convention.
Table forecasts USD/CAD 1.40->1.42 (p.10), directionally consistent with the explicit USD safe-haven strength prose; coherent though minor.
Table-only (-50bp, 4.75->4.25, p.10) with no Australia prose anywhere; a sizeable delta but a call only under the table convention.
Table forecasts AUD 0.75->0.77 (p.10), a ~2.7% appreciation, but the p.3 prose has broad USD safe-haven strength — table 2027 endpoint vs prose near-term stance conflict.

Duplicate of row 18 mapped to a second leaf: the p.8 'Asia' row and the p.10 'Asia ex Japan & China' row are the identical series (5.71->5.67, 4bp), so the model emitted the same trivial delta twice on two leaves — the p.8 'Asia Fixed Income' mapping is the over-mapped one.
Table-only (6.90->6.70, p.10) with no India prose; 20bp delta counts as a call only under the delta-as-view convention.

Table forecasts USD/BRL 5.40->5.20 (p.10, ~3.7% appreciation) while the p.9 prose warns of broad EM currency depreciation under a stronger dollar (GT's EM Basket U) — a direct table-endpoint vs prose-stance conflict.
Same conflict as BRL: table 900->875 (~2.8% appreciation) vs p.9 prose EM currency weakness; both readings are in the source.
Table 18.8->18.5 (~1.6% appreciation) vs the same p.9 EM-FX-weakness prose; the smallest of the three LatAm FX deltas, borderline overreach.
A 10bp delta (5.10->5.00) on a table-only row with no Poland prose is too small to imply a direction even under the model's own convention.
Table-only (8.70->8.90, +20bp, p.10) with no South Africa prose; leaf mapping is correct but the call exists only under the delta convention.

gt_only_root_causes.note: The p.8 Japan section is pure macro prose (energy shock offset by fiscal policy, 1.0% growth); GT inferred an equity stance from country macro — the model emitted zero equity rows for this doc.
Derived from p.5 macro prose (4.5% target, oil reserves cushion, deflation risk); no stated equity view in the source to extract directly.

GT inferred a cash stance from the p.2 Monetary Policy panel ('easing cycle mostly spent... back to a neutral setting'); the 'cash yields attractive' phrasing in GT commentary is analyst gloss not present in the document.
Inferred from p.6 growth prose (energy drag could 'annihilate' Germany's fiscal boost, 0.8% growth); the model captured Europe only as rates/FX rows.
Inferred from the p.7-8 stagflation prose (5.2% unemployment, 75,000 jobs lost, BoE hold); no explicit equity call in the source.

The evidence is explicit in p.9 prose ('stronger US dollar... EM currency depreciation, capital outflows'); the model emitted the USD O side from the same passage but never the EM-FX flip side, instead enumerating per-country table FX deltas that point the opposite way for LatAm.

notes: The two sets barely overlap by construction: the model exhaustively enumerated the p.10 forecast table under a uniform delta-to-view convention at flat confidence 75 (including 4-14bp deltas), while GT authored only prose-grounded macro calls including country equity views the model never attempts. The model also splits one global-duration call across three leaves (Duration, Global Govt Bonds/SSAs, Developed Markets - Sovereigns) and double-maps the identical Asia series to both Asia Fixed Income and Asia ex-China Fixed Income. Where table endpoints and prose stance conflict (EM FX, Europe rates, US 10-yr), the model always follows the table and GT always follows the prose — that single convention choice explains all three exact-match view mismatches and most residual disagreement.
